1359.reason: [The error handling logic is flawed: when a required token (like ';', ')', or ']') is missing, the parser correctly reports an error but fails to simulate consumption of the missing token. This causes subsequent parsing to remain at the same position, potentially leading to infinite loops or cascading errors. The method should always "consume" the expected token conceptually—by not calling move() only when the token is actually present—but the current logic doesn't adjust the parser state appropriately for recovery.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The error handling logic is flawed: when a required token (like ';', ')', or ']') is missing, the parser correctly reports an error but fails to simulate consumption of the missing token. This causes subsequent parsing to remain at the same position, potentially leading to infinite loops or cascading errors. The method should always "consume" the expected token conceptually—by not calling move() only when the token is actually present—but the current logic doesn't adjust the parser state appropriately for recovery.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The method assumes that when the expected token (e.g., SEMICN, RPARENT, RBRACK) is present, it should be consumed via move(), but in many calling contexts, the parser has already constructed a synthetic token and expects the real token to be at current position. However, the logic here only calls move() when the token matches, which may skip over tokens incorrectly or fail to advance when an error is inserted. More critically, this error-handling approach inserts fake tokens into the parse tree without actually verifying or consuming real tokens from the input stream, leading to misalignment between the token stream and parser state.]

1342.reason: [When the expected token (e.g., ';') is present, the parser should not consume it here because the normal parsing logic will handle it. Calling move() here causes the parser to skip the token, leading to missing token processing or incorrect error reporting.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When the expected token (e.g., ';') is present, the parser should not consume it here because the normal parsing logic will handle it. Calling move() here causes the parser to skip the token, leading to missing token processing or incorrect error reporting.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.03333333333333333 , reason:  [This move() call is incorrect - when the expected token is present, we should not advance the token pointer here because the actual token will be processed normally in the parsing logic]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When the expected semicolon is present, the parser should not consume it here; this causes double consumption since the normal parsing flow will also try to consume it]

1349.reason: [Same issue: if ')' is present, it should be processed by normal parsing logic, not consumed here. This premature consumption breaks the parsing flow.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: if ')' is present, it should be processed by normal parsing logic, not consumed here. This premature consumption breaks the parsing flow.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.03333333333333333 , reason:  [This move() call is incorrect - when the expected token is present, we should not advance the token pointer here because the actual token will be processed normally in the parsing logic]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When the expected right parenthesis is present, the parser should not consume it here; this causes double consumption since the normal parsing flow will also try to consume it]

1356.reason: [Same issue: if ']' is present, it should be processed normally, not consumed in error handling. This causes the parser to skip tokens that should be handled by regular grammar rules.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: if ']' is present, it should be processed normally, not consumed in error handling. This causes the parser to skip tokens that should be handled by regular grammar rules.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.03333333333333333 , reason:  [This move() call is incorrect - when the expected token is present, we should not advance the token pointer here because the actual token will be processed normally in the parsing logic]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When the expected right bracket is present, the parser should not consume it here; this causes double consumption since the normal parsing flow will also try to consume it]
